new_classes.definition_en: Grape must classified under CN heading 2204 30 that has been concentrated (e.g. by heating or vacuum evaporation), as defined in additional note 7 to Chapter 22, and which is neither in fermentation nor with fermentation arrested otherwise than by addition of alcohol.
A quality borne by a grape must or juice indicating that it has undergone concentration (reduction of water content), as referenced in additional note 7 to Chapter 22 of the CN.

coverage_explanation: The core distinguishing criterion — that this grape must is "concentrated" (as defined in additional note 7 to Chapter 22) — is captured via the new ConcentratedQuality class and the someValuesFrom restriction on RO:has_quality. The complement restrictions against InFermentationQuality and FermentationArrestedWithoutAlcoholQuality correctly distinguish this subheading from CN 2204 30 10 (in fermentation or arrested without alcohol). However, the precise legal definition from additional note 7 (which likely specifies a minimum Brix level or sugar concentration threshold for "concentrated" grape must) cannot be fully captured without the text of that note, so the coverage is reduced. No container-volume criterion applies at this CN 2204 30 level, which is correctly omitted.
